q: Blood clotting: Which correctly orders the events of hemostasis?
options: Fibrin mesh forms → platelets aggregate → clotting factors released → vessel constricts
Vessel damage → exposed collagen → platelet plug forms → coagulation cascade → fibrinogen converted to fibrin → stable clot
Red blood cells rupture → hemoglobin released → clotting factors activated → wound sealed
Thrombin produced first → vessel dilates → white blood cells arrive → fibrin produced
explanation: Hemostasis: vascular spasm → platelet adhesion to exposed collagen → platelet aggregation (plug) → coagulation cascade (clotting factors) → thrombin converts fibrinogen to fibrin → stable clot.
